audit_items.proof_trace: `auxiliary_device_uninit(adev)` calls `put_device(&adev->dev)`, which only decrements the kobject refcount and does NOT free `adev` memory. Since we are already inside the `.release` callback (refcount=0), the inner `put_device` triggers a refcount underflow WARN via `refcount_t` saturation in this modern kernel, but does NOT re-invoke `adev_release`. The subsequent `kfree(adev->name)` and `kfree(adev)` therefore operate on valid, non-freed memory — no use-after-free occurs.
